seo.title: Building Research Agents with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service research agents

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables building sophisticated Research Agents that leverage Azure OpenAI, Bing Search grounding, and file search for multi-step information gathering and synthesis. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like unvalidated tool outputs, outdated search results, and token limit failures that can compromise accuracy and enterprise compliance. Vex provides middleware validation to ensure reliable, secure agent performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents in Azure AI Agent Service follow a multi-step pipeline: user queries trigger agent threads that invoke tools like Bing Search for web grounding and Azure AI Search for file retrieval. Azure OpenAI models process results through reasoning loops, synthesizing insights while respecting enterprise policies. Agents maintain thread state for iterative refinement, with outputs validated against security controls.

The architecture integrates seamlessly with Azure's ecosystem, using agent projects to define workflows. Common patterns include sequential tool calls for search → analysis → summarization, with grounding data enhancing response accuracy. However, without middleware validation, failures in tool results or token management can degrade performance.

Vex integrates as middleware in the agent processing pipeline, intercepting tool calls and LLM outputs for real-time verification.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
│   User Query    │───▶│ Azure AI Agent   │───▶│   Vex Middleware │
│                 │    │  Thread/Workflow │    │   (Guardrails)   │
└─────────────────┘    │ - Tool Calls     │    └─────────────────┘
                       │ - OpenAI LLM     │             │
                       │ - Grounding      │             ▼
                       └──────────────────┘    ┌─────────────────┐
                                                │ Verified Output │
                                                │   to User       │
                                                └─────────────────┘

Tools: Bing Search ── Azure AI Search ── Synthesis

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents execute Bing Search or file tools without checking outputs against DLP or compliance rules
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware scans tool results with enterprise policy evaluators before LLM processing

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Search results may include stale web content not flagged for recency
risks.mitigation: Vex recency checks validate publication dates and source freshness

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Azure AI Search returns index metadata rather than document content, confusing synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex content extractors normalize file search outputs to pure text

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long research threads hit context limits without summarization or truncation
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers graceful degradation strategies

implementation.title: Create Azure AI Agent Project with Research Workflow
implementation.description: Initialize an agent project with Bing Search and file grounding tools for research tasks

implementation.title: Invoke Research Agent with Multi-Step Query
implementation.description: Run agent thread for complex research requiring iterative tool calls
implementation.code:
from openai import AzureOpenAI

client = AzureOpenAI(
    azure_endpoint="https://your-openai.azure.com/",
    api_key="your-api-key",
    api_version="2024-02-01"
)

response = client.beta.agents.runs.create(
    agent_id="research-agent-id",
    thread_id="thread-123",
    instructions="Research latest AI regulations in EU, cite sources from 2024"
)

print(response.status)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Middleware for Tool Result Validation
vexIntegration.description: Intercept Azure agent tool calls and validate against enterprise policies and recency
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient, PolicyEvaluator

vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-api-key")

def validate_agent_tools(tool_results: dict) -> bool:
    # Check enterprise DLP policies
    dlp_result = vex.evaluate_policy(
        policy="enterprise-dlp",
        content=tool_results["bing_search"]
    )
    
    # Recency check for research data
    recency_result = vex.check_recency(
        sources=tool_results["bing_search"],
        max_age_days=30
    )
    
    return dlp_result.valid and recency_result.fresh

# Integrate in agent pipeline
if not validate_agent_tools(results):
    raise ValueError("Tool results failed Vex validation")

vexIntegration.title: Token Limit Guardrails with Graceful Degradation
vexIntegration.description: Monitor agent thread tokens and trigger summarization before limits
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import TokenMonitor

token_monitor = TokenMonitor(model="gpt-4o", max_tokens=128000)

def process_agent_step(step_input: str, current_tokens: int) -> str:
    if token_monitor.will_exceed(step_input, current_tokens):
        # Graceful degradation
        summary = vex.summarize(step_input, target_tokens=8000)
        return summary
    return step_input

# Wrap Azure agent step processing
processed_input = process_agent_step(latest_step, thread_tokens)
